steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the circle facts first: radii are equal, a diameter sets up a right angle, a tangent is perpendicular to the radius, and central/inscribed angles connect to arcs.

steps.title: Apply the radian-to-degree conversion formula
steps.content:
To convert from radians to degrees, multiply by $\frac{180}{\pi}$. For this angle:
$$\frac{17\pi}{15} \times \frac{180}{\pi}$$

steps.title: Cancel π and compute
steps.content:
The $\pi$ cancels from numerator and denominator:
$$\frac{17 \times 180}{15}$$
Calculate the product: $17 \times 180 = 3060$
$$\frac{3060}{15} = 204$$
The angle measures 204 degrees.
